text.title: External Secrets Operator Recipe Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the External Secrets Operator Recipe methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: ClusterSecretStore MUST use cloud-identity auth (IRSA on EKS, Workload Identity on GKE, OIDC for Vault). Static token auth forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Static token in ClusterSecretStore = static credential in cluster; same breach surface as the keys ESO is supposed to eliminate.

rule.statement: ExternalSecret refreshInterval MUST be calibrated to backend rate limits (default 1h is sensible; <5min for high-rotation secrets like database creds with dynamic generation).

rationale.source: Vault + AWS Secrets Manager rate limits

rule.rationale: Sub-minute refresh hits backend rate limits; hour-long refresh delays rotation by an hour.

rule.statement: ESO RBAC MUST scope each ExternalSecret to a namespace + service account. Cluster-wide secret access via ClusterExternalSecret only with named approver.
rule.rationale: Wide RBAC turns one pod compromise into all-secret exfiltration.

rule.statement: Sync failure MUST page on-call within 5 min: failure metric (eso_externalsecret_sync_calls_error_total) + log pattern. Silent staleness forbidden.
rule.rationale: ESO failures mean secrets go stale; stale secrets cause auth errors that look like bugs.

rule.statement: Each backend secret MUST have a rotation test in runbook: rotate at backend, wait refreshInterval + grace, confirm pod picked up new value via cert/env/health check.
rule.rationale: Untested rotation pipeline often breaks at the pod-restart step.

rule.statement: Mounted secrets MUST go to /var/run/secrets/<name>/ as files, not env vars. App reloads via SIGHUP or polling. Env-var injection requires named approver.
rule.rationale: Env vars persist in process state + crash dumps + logs; file mounts can be re-read on rotation without pod restart.
